fact: Thirty-three miners were trapped approximately 700 metres underground
source: Deep Down Dark: The Untold Stories of 33 Men Buried in a Chilean Mine, and the Miracle That Set Them Free, Héctor Tobar (2014)

fact: The emergency shelter had roughly two days of food supplies intended for only ten people
source: Deep Down Dark, Héctor Tobar (2014); also reported by BBC News and widely documented

fact: Miners rationed about two spoonfuls of tuna every 48 hours during the initial period
source: Deep Down Dark, Héctor Tobar (2014); corroborated by multiple survivor interviews

fact: It took 17 days before a drill probe reached the miners and confirmed they were alive
source: 2010 Copiapó mining accident, Wikipedia (sourced from contemporaneous news reports); contact was made on August 22, 2010, 17 days after the August 5 collapse

fact: The miners spent 69 days trapped underground before all 33 were rescued
source: NASA Engineering and Safety Center involvement documented; rescue completed October 13, 2010 — 69 days after collapse. Widely documented.

fact: The San José mine's owner (Empresa Minera San Esteban) had a history of safety violations and had not properly reinforced access tunnels
source: Chilean government investigation; reported by The New York Times and summarized in the Wikipedia article on the disaster
